prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Alright, listen up. You've been staring at that task all morning, haven't you? It's been sitting there, growing in your mind until it feels like a mountain. But here's the truth: it's not a mountain. It's just a task.

You've put it off because it feels hard, because you're afraid you might not do it perfectly, or because you're worried about how long it might take. But that's just your brain trying to protect you from discomfort. And that's exactly what we're going to push through today.

This task isn't going to get any easier by waiting. In fact, it's probably getting harder as the weight of it builds up in your mind. But you? You're stronger than that weight. You've faced hard things before and you've come out on the other side. This is just another one of those moments.

Right now, I want you to take a deep breath. In through your nose, out through your mouth. Feel that air filling your lungs? That's energy. That's your body saying, "I'm ready."

Now, set a timer for just 15 minutes. That's it. Just 15 minutes of focused work. You don't have to finish the whole thing. You just have to start. Once you start, the momentum will build. One sentence, one step, one small piece at a time. Before you know it, you'll be in the flow, and that mountain will have turned into a hill, then a speed bump, and then you'll be past it.

You've got this. You always have. Now go get it done.
